Criteria: A major release of hazardous materials (chemical, biological, radiological, or explosive) has occurred or is imminent, posing a direct threat to public safety and requiring immediate protective action.
Intentional or accidental release of highly lethal nerve agents, blister agents, or industrial toxic gases (e.g., chlorine, ammonia) requiring immediate life-saving isolation.
An active emergency at a nuclear facility involving core damage or an unmonitored release of radioactive materials into the atmosphere.
The detection or release of radioactive materials outside of a nuclear facility, such as a transportation accident involving medical isotopes or a dirty bomb.
Confirmed or suspected intentional dispersal of harmful biological agents, toxins, or pathogens (e.g., anthrax, ricin) requiring quarantine or specialized decontamination.
An airborne hazard plume (toxic smoke or chemical gas) is passing through the area. Leaving structures would result in hazardous exposure.
Action_Required: Go indoors immediately, close all windows/doors, turn off all ventilation/HVAC systems, and seal gaps.

Criteria: A localized hazardous material spill (e.g., commercial fuel spill, overturned tanker on highway) causing traffic disruptions and minor containment activities, but no immediate broad public threat.
